standard: FDICIA Part 363 — Annual Independent Audits and Reporting Requirements / Internal Controls Over Financial Reporting
description: Mapping of FDICIA Part 363 internal control requirements to AWS Config managed rules and GuardDuty finding types. Applies to insured depository institutions with total assets of $500 million or more.

controls.control_name: Management Assessment — Internal Controls Over Financial Reporting
controls.description: Management must assess the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting, including controls over the safeguarding of assets and the reliability of financial records.
Independent public accountant must attest to and report separately on management's assertions about internal controls. Requires tamper-evident audit evidence chain.
Internal controls must ensure segregation of duties for financial systems. No single user should have unrestricted access to create, authorize, and record financial transactions.
Controls must prevent unauthorized access to financial systems and customer data. MFA required for all console and privileged access.
Root/superuser account usage must be controlled and monitored. Root access key must not exist for any production financial system.

controls.control_name: Data Encryption — Financial Records at Rest
controls.description: Financial records and customer data must be encrypted at rest to prevent unauthorized disclosure and ensure data integrity.

controls.control_name: Data Encryption — Financial Records in Transit
controls.description: Financial data transmitted over networks must be encrypted using TLS. Non-TLS connections to financial systems must be denied.
Network traffic to and from financial systems must be logged and monitored for anomalous activity. VPC flow logs required.
Continuous monitoring controls must detect and alert on threats to financial systems. Automated threat detection required.

controls.control_name: IAM Policy Controls — No Inline Policies
controls.description: Identity and access management controls must follow least-privilege principles. Inline IAM policies bypass centralized governance and must not exist.
Access credentials for financial systems must be rotated regularly. Keys older than 90 days represent a control deficiency under FDICIA internal control standards.
Financial databases must not be publicly accessible. Public RDS instances represent a material weakness in internal controls over financial reporting.
Credentials and secrets used to access financial systems must be automatically rotated. Manual credential management is a control deficiency.
All API endpoints serving financial data must enforce TLS. Unencrypted API traffic to financial systems is a reportable control deficiency.

controls.control_name: S3 Public Access Prohibition — Financial Data
controls.description: S3 buckets containing financial records or audit evidence must block all public access. Public S3 exposure of financial data is a material weakness.
